Salary: + super, bonus, great location and company culture
Ad Text:
Ensure accuracy and reliability of critical RF test equipment through expert calibration, automation and asset management.




WHY BAI COMMUNICATIONS

It’s an exciting time to be part of BAI Communications. Building on our achievements in broadcasting, telecommunications and critical communications, we continue to expand our operations, helping connect people and support communities.

 

We operate one of the world’s most extensive broadcast networks, delivering fully managed television and radio services to 99% of the Australian population. During crises such as natural disasters, national broadcasters depend on BAI to maintain vital communications, while emergency services trust us to keep them connected and informed.




As 5G technology transforms connectivity, BAI is leveraging global experience to enable comprehensive coverage in challenging environments—buildings, tunnels, stadiums, and high-density areas. This expertise positions us at the forefront of intelligent, connected infrastructure development across Australia.




Our acquisition of Titan ICT has strengthened our mission-critical capabilities, adding deep expertise in mining, resources, and energy industries. This expansion enables us to deploy robust solutions across Australia’s most demanding operational environments.




Discover more through BAI360, our virtual tour platform. 




LET’S EXPLORE THE ROLE

As a Calibration Technician, you’ll be responsible for managing and maintaining the accuracy of BAI’s test and measurement equipment fleet. Using reference-standard equipment calibrated by NATA‑accredited laboratories, you’ll ensure lab and field instruments meet required performance standards and are available when teams need them.

This role combines hands‑on calibration work, asset and records management, and opportunities to contribute to automation and continuous improvement across RF Services Engineering.




This role is located in Bald Hills QLD.  We would consider candidates looking to relocate to Brisbane.




Key Responsibilities:

Manage the Test Equipment Resource Plan, including allocation, availability and calibration schedules
Perform calibration of RF test and measurement equipment using manual and automated methods
Maintain calibration certification, traceability and records using MET/TEAM
Develop, document and maintain calibration procedures and workflows
Create and enhance automated calibration routines using SCPI and MET/CAL
Coordinate logistics for calibration, repairs and freight tracking of equipment
Support internal teams with calibration advice and technical queries
Maintain a safe, organised and compliant calibration laboratory environment




LET’S HEAR ABOUT YOU

You are technically hands‑on, detail‑focused and take pride in accuracy and traceability. You enjoy structured work, but also like improving processes and finding smarter ways to do things. Your background enables you to confidently work across a range of RF test environments and support others with practical technical advice.

 

Your experience and capability include:

Practical experience calibrating RF test and measurement equipment such as spectrum analysers, network analysers, oscilloscopes, power meters and test receivers
Sound understanding of calibration principles, standards and traceability
Managing calibration schedules, documentation and certification records
Familiarity with asset management and electronic ticketing systems
Exposure to automation using SCPI, with experience in tools such as MET/CAL advantageous
Ability to interpret technical documentation and follow precise procedures
Comfortable working independently while supporting broader engineering teams

 

Desirable experience:

RF broadcast or telecommunications environments
Scripting or programming exposure (such as Python or C#)
Basic electronic repair capability
A criminal history check is required for this position.




BENEFITS & CULTURE

At BAI, we value diversity and welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally diverse communities, people with disability, and LGBTQIA+ individuals.

Guided by our principles—One BAI Team, Customer First, Adapt and Learn, and Drive Sustainable Growth—we create an environment where you can thrive....

Salary: + super, bonus, great location and company culture

Mid - Senior Data Engineer (Multiple) - Sydney (Hybrid) - $140-170k + Package
  
Nuage has recently partnered with a new client based in Sydney to build out their founding Data & AI capability. You’ll be joining an experienced and well-connected Data & AI leader who has been brought in to establish a high-performing team from the ground up, with a clear goal to position the business as a leader in their field. The team is already working with a range of businesses that focus on modernising their data platforms with AI-driven solutions and shaping how enterprises manage, store, and derive value from their data.
  
What you can expect?
  

An opportunity to be part of a Founding Data/AI practice within a well established business which have honed down on a specific niche in a crowded market
Be part of a business really setting a high bar for creating a world-class Data/AI Engineering practice
Grow with the business as they begin to scale their practice, with plenty of opportunity for career advancement
Investment in you through dedicated L&D budgets and certifications
You'll be working on technology that hasn't even been released yet
Freedom to grow you AI capability
  
What I'm looking for?
  

Strong experience working with Databricks in an Enterprise environment
Prior consultancy experience is highly advantageous
Additional experience with one of the following: Snowflake, BigQuerey, Fabric
Ideally familiar with the Azure stack
Professional Certifications at professional level are highly regarded
Comfortable with hybrid working arrangement (2-3 days on-site)
  
The interview process consists of a stage interview process and are on-going, so apply today to find out more.
  
Mid - Senior Data Engineer (Multiple) - Sydney (Hybrid) - $140-170k base + Package

About Resolution Life

Resolution Life Australasia is one of the leading life insurers in Australia and New Zealand, with ~ 1 million customers with superannuation, investments and life insurance policies, managing approximately A$30 billion in assets and have 1,000 employees across Australia and New Zealand.

We value diverse backgrounds and perspectives, foster an inclusive, collaborative and flexible working environment, where everyone can thrive. We are a fully enterprise agile organisation, and will support your professional growth and reward your contribution.

The Role

We are seeking a technically strong and hands-on Lead Data Engineer to join our Data Operations team. This role is pivotal in designing, delivering, and maintaining robust data solutions that support operational excellence across our enterprise platforms.

The role will focus on end-to-end engineering & maintenance of data pipelines and frameworks, ensuring alignment with architectural standards, governance policies, and business needs. The role will also provide technical leadership and mentorship to engineering and DevOps teams, while actively contributing to support, solution design and delivery.

Key Responsibilities

Build fit-for-purpose data solutions aligned with enterprise architecture and governance

Enforce end‑to‑end Data Operations governance across production data platforms, ensuring operational stability, change compliance, incident management, and alignment to enterprise risk, regulatory, and BAU standards.

Apply agentic AI to anticipate issues, accelerate impact analysis, and support self‑healing data pipelines, maximising reuse of existing data operations knowledge

Create complex SQL queries, data models (3NF, Kimball, Data Vault), and orchestration frameworks (Control-M, Autosys)

Develop batch, near-real-time, and real-time pipelines using ELT/ETL tools like IICS, Informatica PowerCenter, and DataStage

Define patterns for data ingestion, observability, and consumption

Maintain technical data lineage across systems and platforms

Support metadata management, business glossary alignment, and compliance reporting, including customer master and reference data management

Work with stakeholders on solution design, cost-benefit analysis, and roadmap planning

What you bring:

Proven expertise in designing, operating, and supporting end‑to‑end data pipelines and orchestration patterns in complex production data environments

Expertise in advanced SQL, data architecture, and data warehousing methodologies (e.g. SCD, Data Vault).

Demonstrated capability to perform fast, accurate impact analysis in high‑pressure, time‑critical environments, leveraging creative problem‑solving

Strong knowledge of cloud platforms and RDBMS/OLAP systems (Snowflake, SQL Server, Oracle etc.)

Strong understanding of incident management tools and processes, including platforms such as ServiceNow.

Familiarity with data governance frameworks, master and reference data management, and regulatory compliance requirements

Proven ability to lead delivery of data engineering solutions in cross-functional teams and influence technical direction

Demonstrated experience with scripting languages such as Unix Shell, PowerShell, Python, and others

Strong understanding of orchestration methods for batch, near real-time, and real-time processes

Previous experience in automating data operations across multiple platforms is preferred

Why Join Us?

At Resolution Life Australasia, we provide a flexible work environment and access to a range of employee benefits that will support you both personally and professionally including:

Career pathways, including local and global mobility opportunities

Leading Learning Platforms and support in professional development

Health and Wellbeing Programs

Accessibility and Checks
Before commencing employment in this role, you will need to provide two references, full working rights and complete police and credit checks through an online provider. As an equal opportunity employer strongly committed to working in a diverse and inclusive workforce you will be provided with any support or accessibility requirements throughout your interview process. Please feel free to contact our Talent Team directly at .

Privacy Policy

Please refer to our Privacy Policy to learn about how we use the information you give us, alternatively you can view the same information by navigating to the page https://www.resolutionlife.com.au/privacy.

About CTM 
CTM is a global award-winning provider of innovative and cost-effective travel management solutions to the corporate, events, leisure and loyalty travel markets. CTM provides personalised service excellence with client-facing technology solutions in offices all around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, Asia, North America and Europe with over 3000 employees.
  
At CTM, our people are our greatest asset. We are a team of collaborative, innovative and future focused professionals we work with the company's values in mind; Connect, Deliver and Evolve.
  
About the Role
CTM is building the intelligence infrastructure that will underpin AI-powered capabilities across its global travel platform. We are looking for a Principal Engineer, AI Platform to lead that effort.

This is a senior technical leadership role with real organisational scope. You will lead the CTM Intelligence Pillar, spanning an AI/ML platform team and an experimental conversational AI team, and own the technical direction of CTM's shared AI/ML platform: the governed, self-service infrastructure that other engineering teams build on top of.

The team you are joining has strong technical foundations, and the work already in flight reflects genuine engineering rigor. The priority now is accelerating delivery and getting the broader organisation building on top of what exists.

Equally important is the organisational dimension. CTM is moving toward a model where every engineering team can ship intelligent features through governed, self-service platform capabilities. You will shape how that happens, leading the internal AI Guild and working across the engineering organisation to establish what the platform provides and how teams consume it.

About You

10+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 5 years in senior or principal roles
Demonstrated experience managing and mentoring domain or team-level tech leads in a scaled engineering organisation
A track record of applying AI/ML in production systems, with the judgment to know where it belongs and where it does not
Experience working within or alongside internal developer platform models, with a clear understanding of self-service principles and how to apply them to AI/ML capability
Strong architectural judgment across distributed systems, APIs, and data infrastructure
Building scalable retrieval systems (Vector DBs) across fragmented legacy data.
Proven ability to communicate technical strategy to non-technical stakeholders including at executive level, without losing precision
Implementing automated CI/CD for model testing, versioning, and deployment.
Demonstrable experience with: TypeScript, NestJS, PostgreSQL, Prisma, RabbitMQ, direct integration of LLM provider APIs (Azure AI Foundry, OpenAI, or equivalent) in production systems, and strong knowledge of vector stores/RAG and graph databases such as Neo4j,
Experience driving organisational change where the goal was shifting how teams think and work, not just delivering a project
Hands-on experience with multi-agent frameworks to move beyond simple chatbots toward autonomous business process automation.
Implementing real-time safety, PII redaction, and hallucination checks.
Comfortable operating in an environment where not everything is defined yet and some of the hardest problems are political as much as technical
Bachelor's degree or above in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent experience
Why CTM?  
As a global organisation CTM offers a range of employee benefits that including;

Travel discounts  
Perkbox - Retail, Lifestyle, Entertainment, Health & Wellness discounts
Training and Development opportunities  - online lessons and certificates
Annual Volunteer Day  
x2 Wellness/Chillout Days  
Blended work arrangements with hybrid WFH flexibility 
2 Weeks extra leave - Purchase Leave
Paid Parental Leave
Sonder - EAP Platform 
  
CTM offer a strong, established and sustainable work environment which will support your career development and wellbeing.
  
CTM is committed to the unique contributions of all our people and actively encourage candidates with all abilities and diverse backgrounds to apply. Come work for a global award winning company that values its people, community, and technological innovation.

The Role

The AI & Digital Trainer is a key driver of the firm's digital transformation, sitting within the Digital group and working closely with project, change and technology teams. This role focuses on accelerating the adoption of AI and digital tools across the firm by translating technology into practical, day‑to‑day value for our people. We are recruiting an AI & Digital Trainer for our Melbourne, Sydney and Brisbane offices with hybrid working arrangements available.

As an AI & Digital Trainer, you will:

Design and deliver high‑quality digital learning solutions aligned to business needs
Coach and support partners, lawyers and business services teams in the effective use of AI and digital technologies
Drive firmwide adoption of AI tools including Legora, Microsoft Copilot, HelpMe (Moveworks), and other emerging technologies
Deliver digital mentoring and coaching programs that support real work scenarios
Build trusted‑advisor relationships with stakeholders across all levels of the firm
Partner with Digital, project and change teams to understand end‑user experiences and remove barriers to adoption
Modernise and enhance existing IT training materials, incorporating innovative and self‑paced learning approaches
Monitor and report on AI and digital adoption metrics, translating insights into actionable recommendations
Contribute to continuous improvement of learning resources and digital capability across the firm
Ensure digital practices align with cyber security policies and technology standards

More About You

You are confident working with senior stakeholders and passionate about helping people adopt new ways of working through technology.

You will bring:

Professional qualifications in training, learning design or a related discipline
5 years + demonstrated experience designing and delivering eLearning and digital learning solutions
Experience coaching professionals in the adoption of AI and digital tools, ideally in a professional services environment
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365, AI‑enabled tools and enterprise applications
Understanding of change management principles (e.g. ADKAR or equivalent) and experience managing resistance to change
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with confidence in public speaking
Proven stakeholder engagement and consulting skills, with the ability to extract and translate workflow insights
Strong problem‑solving skills and a customer‑focused mindset
Ability to interpret adoption and usage data and turn insights into practical recommendations
A proactive, self‑starter approach with the ability to manage competing priorities




How to apply

We encourage applications from people of all ages, abilities, cultural backgrounds, genders (including trans or gender diverse), LGBTQ+ people and those with carer responsibilities. We particularly encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people to apply.

We prefer to connect with people directly, so please submit your CV by clicking on the 'Apply' button. We encourage all applications, including if you do not meet all the criteria listed for the role. Your application will also enable us to consider you for other opportunities that may be available at MinterEllison.

If you are currently a MinterEllison employee, please apply through the internal careers page.

If you would like further information, require any adjustments throughout the recruitment process or for a confidential discussion, please contact Alexandra.Hussain@minterellison.com.

AI Engineer – Build the Future of AI at Orion

 

We have an exciting opportunity for an AI Engineer to join The Orion Group, a growing market-leading business known for its collaborative culture, strong reputation, and commitment to innovation.

Based in our Norwest office (just a short walk from Norwest Metro) or Sydney CBD, this role offers the chance to work across a variety of projects within a supportive, high-performing team environment, while delivering real impact across the business.

 

Orion is establishing a structured AI function to improve productivity, reduce project administration, and deliver clearer, more consistent transparency for our clients. This role sits at the centre of that vision.

We are seeking a mid–senior AI Engineer / AI Solutions Lead to take ownership of AI solution design and delivery across the business. This is a rare opportunity to build and shape AI capability from the ground up, forming the foundation of Orion’s long-term AI strategy.

 

This is a hands-on, delivery-focused role, suited to someone who enjoys working closely with the business to solve real problems and scale practical, high-impact solutions.

In this role, you will:

Design and implement AI solutions that drive measurable productivity improvements

Work closely with leaders and project teams to identify inefficiencies and deliver practical outcomes

Build, test, and scale AI tools and workflows across the organisation

Contribute to how AI is embedded, governed, and adopted across the business

As Orion continues to grow, so will this role — offering a clear pathway from hands-on delivery to owning the AI roadmap, leading initiatives, and influencing strategic decision-making.

 

Why this opportunity stands out

Build something from the ground up – play a key role in shaping Orion’s AI capability

Real impact – your work will directly improve operations and client outcomes

Strong team environment – collaborative, supportive, and forward-thinking

Practical innovation – apply AI to real business challenges, not just theory

 

This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to step into a role with ownership, visibility, and genuine impact, within a business that is growing, evolving, and investing in the future.

 

Who we are

Orion Group is a dynamic multi-disciplinary land development company that has become known for providing exceptional value to our clients. We are rapidly growing business with five offices across NSW and Queensland. We are a diverse team of project managers, engineers, surveyors, and planners and have recently surpassed the 130-employee milestone.

We use our deep understanding of development to drive innovative solutions for complex projects. We continue to grow as a direct result of our expertise, innovative attitude, quality of service and, solid business practices and a culture to expand horizons. We are market leaders in our industry because of our vision and commitment to integrity, quality, and reliability.

Our vision is to be Australia’s leading source of professional services advice in our sector. We will change what it means to be a professional in our industry and we hope you will join us and play a part in achieving our goal.

 

Responsibilities

AI solution design and delivery

Design and develop AI-enabled solutions that improve productivity and reduce manual administration.

Build, test, and refine prompts and workflows for tools such as Microsoft Copilot.

Assess existing platforms, automation tools, or software before proposing custom solutions.

Develop prototypes and pilot initiatives, then support broader rollout.

Business engagement

Partner with AI Champions in each Business Unit to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities.

Analyse workflows, systems, and data flows across project delivery and support functions.

Propose practical, scalable solutions aligned with business priorities.

Implementation and scaling

Support deployment of AI tools and workflows across teams.

Assist with integration into existing systems where appropriate.

Define and track success metrics such as time saved, accuracy, adoption, and operational impact.

Change and capability uplift

Support training and adoption of AI tools across the organisation.

Develop guidance, templates, and best-practice standards for AI usage.

Build confidence and understanding of AI across technical and non-technical teams.

Client transparency initiatives

Contribute to AI-enabled client reporting, dashboards, and automated updates.

Improve consistency and visibility of project information across the client lifecycle.

Governance and responsible AI

Work within governance frameworks covering data privacy, security, and AI risk.

Support compliant, ethical, and secure use of AI aligned with organisational risk appetite. 

Growth opportunity

This role is designed to grow with the organisation. As AI adoption scales, responsibilities may expand to include:

Ownership of Orion’s AI roadmap and prioritisation framework

Leadership of the AI Champions network

Oversight of AI governance and standards

 

What we’re looking for

Experience

Experience in applied AI, prompt engineering, automation, or AI-enabled solution design.

Hands-on exposure to Microsoft Copilot, Power Platform, or similar productivity-focused AI tools.

Experience working across business functions to design and implement operational solutions.

Coding and software development experience value.

Capabilities

Strong ability to translate business problems into clear, practical solutions.

Comfortable prototyping, testing, and iterating in live environments.

Strong communication skills with the ability to explain AI concepts clearly to non- technical stakeholders.

Analytical mindset with experience mapping workflows and identifying inefficiencies

Mindset

Practical and outcome oriented.

Comfortable operating in a growing and evolving AI environment.

Strong sense of responsibility around data security and ethical AI use.

Collaborative and credible when working with senior stakeholders.

Why Orion

Opportunity to build an AI enablement function from the ground up.

Clear pathway from hands-on delivery to strategic leadership.

Direct exposure to real operational challenges and executive stakeholders.

Focus on meaningful productivity and client outcomes

 

Employee Benefits

Professional development, mentoring & coaching

A range of flexible work options

Community days and cultural celebrations

Access to innovative technology

Employee assistance program

Annual performance appraisal and learning & development plans

Regular team-building activities, social activities, and team sports

Paid parental leave

 

Orion Group highly values diversity and is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from all suitably qualified candidates. This role is open to Australian Citizens and Permanent Residents only (Including New Zealand Citizens). If this exciting opportunity has captured your interest, please submit your application now, via the apply button.

Are you an Automation Test Engineer ready to shape quality from the ground up in a high-performing engineering team?

AutoGuru is an award-winning Australian technology company and FleetGuru is our flagship B2B fleet management platform, now at the forefront of AI-powered fleet intelligence. As we scale FleetGuru nationally and internationally and accelerate our AI roadmap, quality engineering is a core part of how we build, not an afterthought.

We're looking for an Automation Test Engineer to join our Technology team and help us embed quality earlier, deeper and smarter across everything we ship.

What you will be doing:

As an Automation Test Engineer, you'll work as an embedded quality partner within our Engineering team, sitting alongside developers, not separate from them.

This is a hands-on, technical role. You’ll build automation frameworks, get your hands on Playwright and leverage AI tools like Claude Code, and help us ship features the team is genuinely proud of.

Build and maintain E2E test suites using Playwright, growing coverage as the FleetGuru platform accelerates.

Perform API, GraphQL, and integration testing as a core part of every feature delivery.

Use Claude Code to turbocharge test authoring and build reusable, scalable testing frameworks.

Collaborate with developers and BAs to define acceptance criteria before a single line of code is written.

Contribute to CI/CD pipelines so automated tests are baked into every build.

Champion shift-left testing: partner with devs to catch issues earlier and reduce manual regression.

Select, implement, and maintain the testing tools that keep our engineering engine purring.

Keep test repositories clean, fast, and meaningful; no flaky tests allowed in this garage.

About You:

IT degree or comparable industry experience.

Proven experience designing and building automation test suites.

Hands-on experience with Playwright for E2E test automation.

Strong understanding of the SDLC and where quality fits across it.

Hands-on experience with API and GraphQL testing.

Comfortable working alongside developers and contributing to engineering conversations.

Experience with CI/CD pipelines and integrating tests into build processes.

Curiosity about AI and how tools like Claude Code can accelerate and enhance the quality engineering workflow.

Strong communication skills, you can translate quality thinking into language developers and stakeholders both understand.

Based on the Gold Coast or willing to relocate.

Bonus if you've worked with:

AWS cloud platform.

Performance and scalability testing.

AI-assisted development tools, including using Claude Code or similar for test generation and automation.

Shift-left or BDD/TDD methodologies.

Perks: We're an inclusive and flexible workplace, and our perks and culture are pretty sweet (if we say so ourselves). We offer:

Hybrid work options once fully trained.

Take advantage of discounted car servicing and even enjoy a paid day off on your birthday - because you deserve it!

Opportunity to excel in your career through ongoing learning and development from our internal ‘Guruversity’ program.

Bask in the contemporary charm of our industrial office, just a stone's throw away from the beach and public transport, complete with cozy chillout spaces and all-around good vibes.

Park your worries (and your wheels) with free secure parking, bike racks, surfboard racks, and even a pool table for those well-deserved breaks.

Dive into a calendar packed with exciting team events led by our dedicated Culture Crew.

We build high-performing teams aligned to our values of 'here to help', 'move with purpose', 'think 10x', 'play as a team', 'numbers over hype', and 'innovative for the future'.

How to Apply: Submit your resume and cover letter outlining why you're the best fit for the role. Applications close at 5 pm on Thursday, 28th May.

We believe diverse perspectives lead to better decisions and stronger outcomes. We welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and people of all backgrounds and experiences.

LinkedIn: @AutoGuru
Instagram: @autoguru
Facebook: @autoguru.com.au

About MSA National

MSA National is an Australian legal and technology company and the country’s largest outsource provider of mortgage documentation and settlement services to major financial institutions. Operating from offices in Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane, we handle high volumes of settlements daily and continue to grow. Our people, our systems and our service standards are the foundation of everything we do.


The Opportunity

AI represents the most significant strategic opportunity in front of our business this decade. We are establishing a dedicated AI function, sitting outside of technology and reporting directly to the Executive, to drive this agenda with the rigour and focus it deserves.

As Head of AI & Analytics, you will own the strategy, the use-case portfolio, the adoption metrics and the executive-level narrative. You will also hold accountability for our data and analytics foundations — the infrastructure that underpins effective AI deployment. This is a strategic and commercial leadership role: we have engineering capability for the build; we need you for the thinking, the prioritisation and the orchestration.

You will work alongside our AI Change Lead, who manages the people side of the programme, and report directly to the Executive, providing regular reporting on where adoption is progressing, where value is being realised and where the business should focus next.


What You'll Be Doing

•       Own the AI strategy for MSA National — determining what we build, what we procure, what we partner on, and in what sequence

•       Lead the AI function: surface use cases from across the business, prioritise them against value and risk, and manage them from concept through to production

•       Maintain governance oversight of all AI tools and automation in use across the business, ensuring visibility, monitoring and accountability

•       Own our data warehouse, business intelligence reporting and analytics roadmap; ensure our AI programme has the data foundations it requires

•       Partner with the AI Change Lead on staff training, AI tool rollout and internal capability-building

•       Partner with IT, Risk and the CISO on deployment governance, risk controls and responsible AI practices

•       Evaluate and run proof-of-concept engagements with AI vendors and partners, bringing commercial rigour to selection decisions

•       Present regular AI programme reporting to the Executive team, covering adoption, use cases in progress, value delivered and emerging risks

•       Monitor the external AI landscape and provide the Executive with timely intelligence on developments relevant to our business and sector





What You'll Bring

Essential

•       7+ years in a senior analytics, data or AI leadership role, ideally within a mid-sized financial services, legal services or shared-services business

•       A genuinely strategic mindset — experienced in portfolio prioritisation and connecting AI investment to measurable business outcomes

•       Deep AI fluency — able to engage credibly with engineers on predictive, generative and agentic AI, while operating primarily as a strategist and orchestrator rather than a practitioner

•       Strong commercial instincts — you connect every use case back to cost-to-serve, revenue, speed or customer experience

•       Hands-on familiarity with data infrastructure — data warehouses, BI platforms and a sound understanding of data quality requirements

•       Exceptional communication skills — equally effective briefing an executive team, authoring a policy document and engaging a frontline team

•       Experience managing external vendor and partner relationships, including proof-of-concept to production processes


Desirable:

•       Hands-on experience building and deploying AI agents or automation tools

•       Background in mortgage, conveyancing, legal or PEXA-adjacent businesses

•       Completion of a structured AI leadership programme

•       Prior experience establishing an AI or analytics function from the ground up




 What We Offer

•        A senior leadership role with direct Executive sponsorship and broad organisational visibility

•       The opportunity to design and build the AI function from first principles, rather than inherit an existing one

•       An Executive team that is genuinely committed to the AI agenda and actively engaged in its success

•       Modern Macquarie Park offices and the scale of a national business

•       Competitive base salary and short-term incentive, a professional development budget, and a clear mandate to reshape how we operate


How to Apply

Please apply via SEEK with your CV and a brief cover note. We would welcome your reflections on one AI or analytics initiative you are particularly proud of, and how you measured its impact.

Shortlisted candidates will meet the CEO, then our Head of Operations and CIO, then a small panel from the Executive team. We aim to have the successful candidate in the role within 30 to 45 days.


General Guidelines

All enquiries will be treated in the strictest confidence. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. This is strictly a full-time, work from office position.

Overview

Due to increased demand we have another opening for a Graduate. So if you are a tech-savvy graduate looking to kickstart your career in the exciting world of artificial intelligence, automation, data or digital? Blackbook.AI, a leading technology consulting company, is seeking ambitious graduates to join us asap.

What you'll be doing

As part of our team, you'll have the opportunity to work within one or more of the following practices:

Data Science: Our team leverages Classical ML, Deep Learning, Neural Networks to solve customer problems.

About InModal 

Our people are experts in public transport technology and major complex program delivery. We have partnered up with INIT, who are a global leading provider of integrated smart ticketing solutions for public transport, as their delivery partner here in Australia. 

Some new and fantastic news!

We have recently been awarded the prestigious high-profile contract to deliver the Opal Next Generation (ONG) Program for Transport NSW.  The ONG Account Based Ticketing program seeks to upgrade the existing gates, poles and bus validator infrastructure, integrate advanced technologies, and significantly enhance user experience across various modes of NSW public transport.  This program is set to run over 13 years, with an initial design and build phase of 3 years through to the operation and maintenance phase which will continue for another 10 years.


The Opportunities 

We're looking for several Systems Engineers to support delivery of a complex back-office technology solution within this major program. 

You'll play a key role across requirements, system design, and verification, ensuring solutions are robust, traceable, and aligned to customer needs. 
This is an opportunity to work in a structured engineering environment while contributing to a system that supports Account-Based Ticketing (ABT) for public transport. 

Our initial 3 roles will involve specialising across either validator, gate or back-office technologies. 

Key Responsibilities 


Analyse and manage system requirements, ensuring clear traceability from customer needs through to system specifications 

Contribute to system design documentation, including functional specifications and interface definitions 

Develop and maintain requirements traceability across the lifecycle 

Support verification and validation activities, including defining acceptance criteria and test cases 

Participate in design reviews, trade studies, and technical decision-making 

Ensure quality, completeness, and consistency of engineering documentation 

Maintain alignment with systems engineering processes and governance frameworks 

Collaborate with engineering, integration, and test teams to support delivery 

 
About You 
You're a detail-oriented Systems Engineer who enjoys working across the lifecycle and contributing to well-structured engineering outcomes. 
You will bring: 


Around 5 years V Modal Systems Engineering experience in requirements management and system design 

Systems Engineering across hardware and software devices 

Experience with complex, multi-system environments 

Understanding of systems engineering lifecycle and processes (e.g. ISO 15288, INCOSE) 

Strong analytical and problem-solving capability 

Ability to produce clear, structured technical documentation 

Experience with tools such as Jama, Doors or Polarion or similar 

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills 

 
Desirable Experience 


Exposure to transport, payments, or ticketing systems ideal but not essential - will also consider industry experience from Defence, Aviation, Control systems or Medical (devices). 

Familiarity with SysML / UML modelling 

Qualifications: 


Degree in Engineering, Computer Science or related discipline 

INCOSE certification (ASEP, CSEP, or equivalent) desirable 

Why InModal?

You’ll be working with a global industry leader on a new high-profile, prestigious project, shaping the future of public transport.   Our work directly improves quality of life in cities by enabling reliable, efficient, and sustainable public transport systems that benefit both communities and the environment.

We are committed to delivering innovative infrastructure solutions that create long-term value and support more connected, liveable cities.

At InModal, you’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact in a growing, evolving business that values initiative and fresh ideas.  You’ll be part of a supportive environment that combines the stability of an established organisation with the energy and ambition of a company on the rise.

Benefits include


Flexible and hybrid working hours (40% from home)

An extra paid annual leave day per year

An extra paid day off for volunteer work

Company bonus

About DBG Health
At DBG Health, we are Defined by Good. Guided by a simple but powerful purpose: to create a world where feeling better is a right, not a privilege. Every day, millions of people rely on us for access to affordable, innovative health, wellness, and beauty solutions that make a genuine impact. Headquartered in Melbourne, we are a global-scale, privately owned organisation, powered by over 1,500 passionate professionals, united by a mission to help people feel better, live well, and thrive. 
  
The Operations division brings together Information Technology, Supply Chain, Finance, the Project Management Office, and Scientific Affairs to support and enable all key divisions across the group. Operating at both strategic and operational levels, our operations team ensure seamless systems, robust governance, efficient supply chains, and scientific integrity, empowering DBG Health’s divisions to perform at their best and deliver exceptional outcomes for customers, partners, and patients alike. 


About The Role:
  
As a Data Engineer, you'll be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining scalable data pipelines and platforms to deliver reliable, high-performing data for analytics and decision-making. Manage the end-to-end data lifecycle, from ingestion to modelling, while leveraging cloud, data lakes, and distributed frameworks.

 

Collaborate with engineers and stakeholders to deliver high-quality, governed data solutions, enabling secure, reusable, and accessible data for both centralised and self-service use.
 
  
Role & Responsibilities:

Create and optimise ETL/ELT pipelines using Azure Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, and Azure SQL Database to deliver scalable, high-performance data solutions.
Develop and maintain data models, stored procedures, and performance-optimised queries; implement architectural improvements and hotfixes to enhance the enterprise data platform.
Implement automated testing, deployment, and monitoring of data workflows using Azure DevOps to ensure reliability and efficiency.
Translate business requirements into robust Azure-based data solutions, working closely with analysts, product owners, and business teams.
Define and enforce data governance standards, ensure data integrity, and maintain accurate and accessible enterprise data for decision-making.
Maintain technical documentation, metadata repositories, audit trails, and runbooks to support compliance, platform sustainability, and business continuity.
Skills & Experience:

2–5 years designing and developing ETL/ELT processes, optimising SQL Server applications, and supporting relational database environments.
Proficiency with Microsoft technologies including SQL Server, Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Lake, and related services; strong knowledge of dimensional and relational data modelling techniques.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions, communicate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders, and troubleshoot data anomalies with strong analytical skills.
Experience with Python, cloud-native platforms (Azure Synapse, Databricks), and DevOps practices including CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and deployment in Azure DevOps is highly regarded.
Understanding of data governance, metadata management, master data management (MDM) is desirable, as well as experience implementing frameworks for data lineage, cataloguing, and quality monitoring.
Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Data Engineering, BI, IT, or related field; relevant Microsoft certifications (DP-203, DP-900, DP-300, DP-700) and additional cloud, DevOps, or security certifications are advantageous.
Benefits   

Exclusive employee discounts via the DBG Health Employee Shop, including MCoBeauty, Esmi Skin Minerals, Nude by Nature, e.l.f. Cosmetics, Chemist's Own and more.  
Acacia EAP Wellbeing Support, providing confidential 24/7 counselling, mental health support, financial coaching, and manager assistance.  
Join a values-driven, supportive, and friendly culture within a dynamic, fast-growing business that actively promotes professional development and internal mobility.  
Enjoy the ability to work autonomously in an empowering environment that supports ownership, accountability and personal success.  
Access to the Bastas Academy for Health Leadership (via Melbourne Business School), supporting emerging and senior leaders with industry-leading programs, coaching, and development.  
DBG Health Leadership Academy, offering structured leadership development, mentoring, and clear career progression pathways.  
DBG Health is committed to fostering a workplace culture that is safe and accessible for all. We are an equal- opportunity employer and encourage applications regardless of cultural background, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. We strive to build a collaborative, inclusive workplace that is representative of the communities we work and live in.

O’Brien® Glass is one of Australia’s most trusted household brands, known for delivering exceptional service and quality for over 100 years. With a national footprint and a strong commitment to innovation, we’re modernising the way we use data to support smarter decision making across the business. When you join O’Brien®, you join a team driven by values, collaboration, and a genuine focus on helping our people succeed.

Working closely with the Business Intelligence Manager, you’ll play a key role in improving data quality, automating core processes, and lifting our overall data capability.

Partnering with IT, BI, and business stakeholders, along with external consultants

4–5 years’ experience in Data Engineering or BI 

Strong SQL, ETL/ELT, and data modelling expertise 

Excellent communication and documentation skills 

Strong business acumen and attention to detail 

Bachelor degree and/or relevant vendor certifications 

Nice to Have: 

Snowflake certification 

Experience with Finance, Supply Chain, Service Management, or Call Centre systems 




Why You’ll Love Working at O’Brien® Glass 

Be part of an exciting transformation of our data environment 

Supportive and collaborative BI and IT teams 

Opportunities to influence data processes and deliver meaningful business value

Job Title: Research Scientist (AI and Scientific System Infrastructure)
Category:
Engineering - Software (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About AIMS

The Australian Institute of Marine Science is Australia’s tropical marine research agency.

We are a trusted advisor, a partner and employer of choice, and we value integrity, innovation, collaboration and respect. AIMS’ research of Australia’s tropical marine ecosystems guides their effective environmental management and contributes to our understanding of similar ecosystems around the world.

Our research seeks to deliver practical benefit to all Australians and supports the sustainable use of our tropical marine estate. Governments, reef managers, industry and the wider community look to our high-quality, long-term research for informed decisions about our iconic Great Barrier Reef, North-West Shelf, and Arafura and Timor Seas. AIMS expert teams at our locations in Townsville, Darwin and Perth have access to world-class infrastructure, unique datasets, cutting-edge laboratories, and a fleet of modern research vessels.

About this exciting opportunity

Working within the Data Analytics & Artificial Intelligence (DAAI) Team, this role will be key in developing and operationalising AI/ML models and AI/ML enabled digital twins of coral reefs and associated marine ecosystems to support monitoring, prediction, and scenario analysis across reef, coastal, and shelf environments.

As our Research Scientist (AI and Scientific System Infrastructure) you will bridge the gaps between marine science, AI, and production grade AI/ML engineering by leading and contributing to the design and development of digital twins for marine systems, integrating observational data, simulations, and AI/ML models to support monitoring, prediction, and scenario analysis.

About you

As a Research Scientist you will have an advanced research background and ideally hold a PhD in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Data Science, or a quantitative marine science discipline. You will have a demonstrated expertise in operationalising advanced AI/ML solutions including transitioning complex models such as digital twins, simulations, Generative AI, and Scientific Machine Learning (SciML)—from research environments into reliable, production grade systems.

Our ideal applicant will have experience deploying AI/ML systems using cloud‑native architectures, familiarity with AI governance, and experience in designing interactive dashboards and visual analytics for scientific or decision‑support applications.

Apply now and join a world leading organisation with attractive working conditions.  In line with our Enterprise Agreement, the successful candidate will be rewarded with:

AIMS AOF Level 6 salary ($146,063 to $164,591 per annum) plus 15.4% superannuation
Full-time, Permanent opportunity
Located in Townsville (Qld) or Canberra (ACT)
9-day fortnight, enjoy a long weekend each fortnight
Flexible Work Arrangements considered including hybrid working (where suitable)
Generous and supportive leave entitlements
Access to Commuter car fleet arrangements to travel to site (in Townsville only)
Salary Sacrifice opportunities
Access to Fitness Passport program, offering discounted membership to selected gyms
Employee Assistance Program
Relocation assistance available

Discover more about Working at AIMS | AIMS.

Please note:

Non-Australian Citizens must hold an appropriate Visa with working entitlements that allows paid employment with AIMS from commencement and for the term of the appointment, depending on the relevant Department of Home Affairs current policies. AIMS may be able to consider sponsorship for international applicants for this role, however, please note that visa processing times can impact the ability for AIMS to consider sponsorship for vacancies. AIMS must meet the requirements of the Department of Home Affairs immigration policies and processes, including the necessity to test the local labour market for suitable applicants. This includes international talent pool members who are Australian visa holders who have relevant working rights.

Next Step

Further information about this exciting and challenging opportunity is outlined in the Candidate Information Pack which is available to download from our website www.aims.gov.au. If, after reviewing the position description and selection criteria included in the Candidate Information Pack, you believe that your qualifications, experience and professional capabilities will enable you to successfully the deliver the position responsibilities, we encourage you to make an application via our website.

Submitting your Application: Please ensure that you have your completed documents (selection criteria responses, resume and cover letter) ready to attach to your online application form via our e-recruitment system which can be accessed via our website www.aims.gov.au.

Position enquiries are to be directed to Kamran Shafi, Manager Data Analytics & AI at k.shafi@aims.gov.au.  Applications must be made through our website per above.

Applications close: WEDNESDAY, 27 MAY 2026 (11.45PM, AEST).

Summary

As a Modern Workplace Power Platform & Copilot Consultant, you will be part of our Modern Workplace Team delivering Microsoft technologies and implementations in enterprise environments. You will use your expertise to design and deliver innovative and tailored solutions that focus on the Power Platform & Copilot Studio to meet our clients' needs and maximize their return on investment. We demonstrate innovation and ambitiousness to achieve performance targets and establish Avanade as a leader in Power Platform and as a #1 Employer of Choice.


As a Power Platform & Copilot Studio Consultant, you will design, build, and deliver intelligent low code and AI powered solutions using Microsoft Copilot Studio, Power Apps, and Power Automate. You’ll work directly with enterprise clients to identify use cases, run workshops, and implement secure, scalable copilots and automations that drive measurable business outcomes.




Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and deploy AI-powered copilots and agents using Microsoft Copilot Studio, including topics, orchestration, generative responses, and actions 

Develop and extend solutions across the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse; Power BI exposure desirable) 

Lead client workshops (discovery, envisioning, and enablement) to identify automation and AI use cases

Be responsible for architecting and designing Power Platform tenants/environments, liaising with IT, Security, and other technical groups to ensure all integration needs are met and fully implemented

Analyze and lead discussions around architectural options, ensuring all options are considered for optimal deployment

Integrate copilots with Microsoft 365, SharePoint, Dataverse, Dynamics 365, and external systems using connectors and APIs

Apply governance, security, and ALM best practices, including DLP policies and environment strategies

Produce solution designs, technical documentation, and handover materials for clients

Work with internal and external technical resources to enable the most effective DevOps processes

Collaborate with architects, data, and AI specialists to deliver end to end solutions

Communicate with Microsoft Product Engineering on product related issues, suggesting improvements and participating with other users in the Power Platform community




Skills & Experience

Hands on experience with Copilot Studio (formerly Power Virtual Agents) and the Power Platform

Strong understanding of Power Platform governance, security, and ALM 

Client facing consulting experience, including requirements gathering and stakeholder management

Familiarity with Microsoft 365, Teams, and enterprise integration patterns

Microsoft certifications such as PL 900, PL 200, PL 400, PL 600, AI-900, AB-730, AB-100

Experience with Azure services (Functions, API integrations)

You will likely possess a Bachelor's degree or equivalent in computer science, computer engineering, information system management or a related field.

3–5+ years in Power Platform consulting roles

You’ll need to be an Australian Citizen due to project and client requirements.

You are an experienced technologist and are comfortable working with a technical team with a track record of solution design & delivery.




In addition, your technical skillset likely includes: 

Demonstrated experience interfacing with clients, assessing their goals and objectives, with the ability to translate ideas into strategic and practical solution recommendations

Aptitude for critical and analytical thinking, as well as the capacity to accept out-of-the box solutions

Being proactive in staying up to date on the latest updates to the Power Platform, as well as future roadmap features

Demonstrated experience with Power Apps, Power BI, or Power Automate, as well as experience with M365, Azure, or Dynamics 365

Demonstrated excellence in communicating and presenting complex information to technical and non-technical stakeholders, both verbally and in written form

We are seeking an experienced AI Engineering Lead (MLOps / Machine Learning) to drive the design, delivery, and ongoing operation of production AI solutions in a cloud-based environment.

This is a hands-on leadership role where you will guide a small team while working closely with software, data, and business stakeholders to ensure AI systems are reliable, scalable, secure, and delivering real-world impact.

Important:
This is not a traditional DevOps or API-based AI role. We are specifically seeking candidates with hands-on experience building and training machine learning models, and taking them end-to-end through development, production deployment, and ongoing performance optimisation.

Candidates with only DevOps, cloud engineering, or AI API experience are not suited to this role.

You will lead the delivery of AI and machine learning solutions across the full lifecycle — from model development and training through to deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement in production.

Key Responsibilities
Lead end-to-end AI and machine learning delivery into production
Build, train, evaluate, and deploy machine learning models
Define and embed MLOps standards and responsible AI practices
Monitor model performance, accuracy, and data drift
Architect and maintain scalable ML pipelines and data workflows
Improve model performance through real-world feedback loops
Act as the primary technical contact for AI initiatives
Mentor and guide a small technical team
Collaborate with business and technology stakeholders
What You’ll Bring
3+ years’ experience in AI/ML production environments
Hands-on experience building and training machine learning models (not just APIs)
Strong understanding of model performance, accuracy, and generalisation
Proven experience taking models from development to production
Strong Python skills
Experience with Azure and Docker
Solid understanding of MLOps, CI/CD, and data engineering workflows
Experience mentoring or leading technical team members
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Location: Morwell, West Gippsland & Latrobe Valley VIC
Salary: A great role at our iconic site!
Ad Text:
Work within a supportive and talented team
Full time permanent opportunity
Be a part of one of Australia's most iconic brands!


The Role

Our Morwell site is located about 150km east of Melbourne, manufacturing a large range of products in yoghurt and dairy desserts including brands such as Farmers Union, Dairy Farmers and Yoplait. Operating 24 hours a day, six days a week across 9 lines the site is highly automated and is the largest yoghurt facility in Australia.

We have an excellent opportunity for an experienced Automation Engineer to join the Engineering & Reliability team. You will report to the Automation Leader looking after all the automation and OT systems. You will provide technical expertise to support manufacturing, quality, maintenance, capital projects and continuous improvement activities.

Previous experience in Dairy or Food Manufacturing is necessary to be able to hit the ground running.

Over the next five years, our site has lots of exciting new projects and improvements happening for us to continue being a leader in Yogurt manufacturing in Victoria. You must be a highly motivated, self-driven individual with the ability to prioritise and problem-solve. This role also has an afterhours on call roster once you are up to speed.


Responsibilities

Provide specialist technical support for manufacturing as required
Actively contribute to continuous improvement projects using automation to improve production capability and reliability
Implementation of automation/SCADA/MES/HMI projects and/or specify a scope of works to be implemented by external contractors
Design, implementation and validation of PLC, SCADA, HMI & MES software changes that are required for the automated plant processes
Provide high quality technical documentation for system design, functional specifications, reports, change management, testing & commissioning
Assist & mentor production team members to better understand the automation controls & impacts


About you

Extensive experience within dairy industry or food processing, preferable within the Dairy Industry or other Food Processing Industries
High level of PLC programming expertise (and ideally familiar with Omron, Allen Bradley) and understanding of Proficy Batch (S88)
High level of expertise in SCADA/HMI/MES development & configuration (Proficy iFIX, Omron, Rockwell & Ignition)
High level of IT skills to support and further develop Manufacturing Information Systems (MIS) (and ideally familiar with Proficy Historian, SEEQ, Ignition and underpinning IT technologies)
IT technologies (Windows desktop/server Operating Systems, SQL Server, SQL Server Reporting Services, Active Directory, Internet Information Server, Excel, VBA, Ethernet networks)

Bega Group Story

Bega Group is home to many trusted and iconic food and beverage brands. Whether it's VEGEMITE on toast, a Dare on the go or a Zooper Dooper on a hot day - you've probably grown up enjoying the iconic products we proudly manufacture every day.

Our growth story of becoming a successful ASX 200 listed company with an annual turnover in excess of $3 billion and approximately 4,000 employees, is made possible by our dedicated workforce who live and breathe our values every day. We have a strong regional focus and proudly support the communities where we work, live and play. At the Bega Group, we are proud of our heritage and will continue to build great opportunities to encourage our people to develop and thrive. Come and be a part of our success story as we continue to build The Great Australian Food Company through creating Great Food for a Better Future!


Life at Bega Group

Company culture and our values are very important to us. From flexible working arrangements and regular social catch ups, we're committed to fostering a supportive and agile work environment for our team.

Join a dedicated, forward-thinking and innovative culture
Grow with us, through opportunities for career development and progression
Contribute by giving back to your community through charities we partner with
Access to mental and physical health programs through our wellbeing portal and programs
Enjoy excellent employee benefits, including flexible working, purchased leave options and salary continuance insurance


Applications

The successful applicant will be required to complete a pre-employment medical.

At Bega, diversity and inclusion is a business priority, we want everyone to be themselves regardless of their background, religion, sexual orientation, age or gender so we can truly reflect the customers and communities that we serve. No matter how you identify, we encourage talented people from all backgrounds, abilities and identities to apply for roles at Bega.

We will accept applications from all people with the right to live and work in Australia.

This niche financial services group is seeking an AI implementation manager.

The AI Implementation manager is responsible for deploying agentic AI and automation technology across the business. The role exists to accelerate adoption, identify and prioritise high-value use cases, establish appropriate AI governance and infrastructure, and build internal capability so teams can operate independently after the engagement ends. Success is measured by deployed automations in production, team readiness, and a clear handover plan for ongoing ownership.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills will be essential to allow the establishment of effective working relationships across the organisation.

You will have relevant tertiary qualifications, demonstrated experience deploying AI agents, automations, and LLM-based solutions in a business environment. You will have the ability to be hands on whilst have a strategic outlook. Strong familiarity with multiple LLM platforms (Claude/Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) and their comparative strengths.

For further information- Please Call Jeremy Gibb on 0438 398 327 or email jgibb@peopleone.com.au

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing 

 

As an AI Engineer, you will provide technical leadership and expertise, driving the design, development, and delivery of innovative AI solutions for the Aggregation business. 

 

You will ensure technical excellence through modern AI development practices, data driven thinking, strategic technology use, and alignment with business goals. This role actively contributes to architectural decisions, promotes best practices, and supports a culture of continuous innovation with the business. As well as:

 

Own AI feature delivery: work from discovery to production; define technical approaches, identify clear entry and exit points, and assist with rollout plans for new AI capabilities.
Build with AI: design prompts; orchestrate LLMs; implement retrieval augmented generation and memory; integrate with model context protocol; ensure observability; support evaluations; build a repeatable AI delivery lifecycle.
Lay a solid foundation: influence key decisions on the future of AI in the Aggregation Business; establish AI engineering standards (prompt patterns, guardrails, observability, evaluation, cost optimisation); DevOps for AI artefacts; document decisions and trade-offs; ensure alignment with company AI policies
Mentor and multiply: coach engineers on AI and productionising AI solutions.
Partner with product, data and engineering teams:  translate customer needs into measurable AI outcomes; instrument usage, quality, reliability; iterate based on data.

                                                                                                                                      

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

 

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program

 

What Sets You Apart 

5+ years of experience in full-stack environments.
Hands-on experience taking AI features to production under real world constraints: scale, latency, cost and regulatory risk.
Demonstrated expertise in system design and integration, including foundation model selection, agentic framework choice, multi-modal input handling (structured data, text, and speech), RAG pipelines (chunking, embeddings, vector stores), and MCP.
Proficiency in Python essential.  Proficiency in other languages (Typescript/JavaScript, C#, Java, SQL) desirable
Experience with AWS Cloud (including Bedrock), Databricks, Kubernetes, serverless, event-driven architectures.
Proficient in modern DevOps practices, observability, and end-to-end software development essential.
Curiosity and willingness to adapt in a fast-moving field essential
Bonus: Include in your CV the exciting things you've deployed in AI

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect we’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and we embrace diversity.  

 

This role is available to candidates with unlimited Australian working rights only. Sponsorship is not available.

Our world is transforming, and PTC is leading the way. Our software brings the physical and digital worlds together, enabling companies to improve operations, create better products, and empower people in all aspects of their business.

Our people make all the difference in our success. Today, we are a global team of nearly 7,000 and our main objective is to create opportunities for our team members to explore, learn, and grow - all while seeing their ideas come to life and celebrating the differences that make us who we are and the work we do possible.

Onshape by PTC is the world's first and most widely used cloud-native CAD and PDM software for professional mechanical design engineers. Engineering.com has recognized it as the fastest-growing CAD system, and Onshape offers an exciting opportunity to help expand its footprint over North America.

Role Overview:

We are seeking an energetic and highly skilled individual to join the Onshape Technical Services team. The ideal candidate is articulate and experienced in delivering engaging product demonstrations. You will collaborate with a fast-paced, in-office team at the PTC HQ in Boston's Seaport District, supporting our sales efforts by showcasing our product to potential new customers. This role demands a strong understanding of engineering applications (CAD, PDM, PLM, CAM, FEA, etc.) and the ability to effectively communicate our product's unique features. You will play a crucial role in introducing a next-generation product design platform to a broader audience. 

Key Responsibilities:

Master our product and become an expert user.

Collaborate closely with the sales team to deliver exceptional product demonstrations.

Act as a product expert, addressing questions from customers and prospects.

Develop technical product-related marketing content.

Expand our ever-growing library of demonstration content.

Liaise with the development team to relay feature requests from customers and prospects.

Continuously enhance your skills and knowledge.


Required Skills:

Experience with mechanical CAD software (Onshape, SOLIDWORKS, Autodesk Inventor, Creo, Siemens NX, etc.)

Experience in delivering product demonstrations.

Experience in troubleshooting complex customer requirements.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical, Industrial, or Electrical Engineering, or a technical equivalent.

Preferred Skills:

Experience and familiarity with PDM or PLM products (SOLIDWORKS PDM, Windchill, Autodesk Vault, Siemens Teamcenter)

Experience with engineering software such as CAM, FEA/CFD, ECAD.

All Orbits, All Planets - Join us on our journey

Gilmour Space is a leading Australian space company, developing launch vehicles, satellite platforms, advanced propulsion technologies; and launching from our licensed orbital spaceport in Bowen, North Queensland.

Are you a creative and experienced Systems Modelling Engineer II (Thermofluids) looking for a dynamic role where your skills will make a real impact? Have you ever dreamt of building rockets right here in Australia? Well now’s your chance! Gilmour Space is seeking a talented individual to join our innovative team to send rockets to Space! Conveniently located between Brisbane and the Gold Coast, our new state-of-the-art facility, purpose-built for designing and manufacturing rockets in-house, is equipped with the latest technology and tools, providing an ideal environment for innovation and excellence. So, what are you waiting for? Join us and be a part of a forward-thinking team dedicated to pushing the boundaries of space exploration and let’s send Australia to Space together!

How you will contribute to our mission in getting Australia to space:

The below is designed to give you an idea of the role you will play. There will be many more exciting and innovative things to do:

Conduct and support systems modelling in all phases of our products life cycle and participate in Systems Engineering reviews.
Conduct high and low-fidelity aerodynamic analyses and advise on aerodynamic design.
Determine aerothermal heating flight loads for launch vehicles.
Perform system-level thermal modelling to define boundary conditions for subsystem thermal models.
Execute high-level propulsion system modelling to inform propulsion architecture and derive system-level performance parameters.
Manage propellant loading and consumable masses to establish their component of the vehicle and satellite gravimetrics.
Model propellant slosh frequencies, damping, and resulting forces within tanks.
Apply data analysis techniques to validate models from raw test data.
Work alongside the Systems Engineers providing analysis and recommendations that assist with requirements definition, verification of system level requirements, trade studies, and engineering change requests.
Review and audit work conducted by the engineering departments.
Operate as part of a multidisciplinary team - routinely step beyond core duties to tackle cross-domain tasks and coordinate with other engineering departments.
Other duties as requested by Management.
Minimum Qualifications, Skills and Experience:
Bachelor's degree in Physics, Mechanical or Aerospace / Aeronautical Engineering or a relevant qualification.
Experience with analytical modelling of fluid systems.
Experience with thermal modelling.
Experience with programming in Python, MATLAB, or other high-level languages.
Ability to operate in dynamic, fast paced environments where roles / responsibilities change rapidly.
Ability to work efficiently in a team environment with a desire and willingness to assist other team members.
Ability to obtain NV1 Security Clearance.
Experience with CFD and its application to a range of problems, including high-speed flows.
2+ years’ experience in engineering analysis and modelling.
Desired Qualifications, Skills and Experience:
Working knowledge of the propulsion industry
Excellent technical writing and verbal communication skills.
Exceptional time management, communication, and interpersonal skills.
Experience with git workflows.
Experience with software verification and validation, as well as CI/CD pipelines.
Basic understanding of systems engineering fundamentals.

How to Apply:

Applicants must be an Australian Citizen/Permanent Resident or have an appropriate Australian work visa or work entitlements.

If you are a proactive team player with a passion to get Australia to space and a desire to succeed, please apply now by clicking the apply button and including your resume and cover letter or head to our careers page: www.gspace.com/career

Gilmour Space Technologies is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members. We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples for all positions.
